SwedishNote: GROUP: 1 # FORM: Infinitiv # FREQ: 10 # NUANCE: To show or demonstrate something to someone either visually or by explanation. It is a fundamental verb for communication and direction. # PART: Visa upp (show off/present), visa ut (show someone out) # COLL: Visa vägen, visa hänsyn, visa respekt, visa intresse # REG: Neutral and used in all contexts from daily speech to formal writing. # REFL: Visa sig (to appear or turn out to be) # ERR: Often confused with 'peka' (to point). Students might also use 'showa' as a Swenglish mistake.
PresentNote: GROUP: 1 # FORM: Presens # FREQ: 10 # NUANCE: Describes an ongoing action or a general fact where someone is currently displaying or demonstrating something. # PART: Visar upp, visar runt (show someone around) # COLL: Han visar vägen, statistiken visar att # REG: Universal register # REFL: Det visar sig att (It turns out that) # ERR: Forgetting the -r ending which is common for Group 1 verbs in present tense.
PreteritumNote: GROUP: 1 # FORM: Preteritum # FREQ: 9 # NUANCE: Refers to a completed action in the past where something was shown or demonstrated at a specific time. # PART: Visade upp, visade bort (dismissed/showed away) # COLL: Visade leg (showed ID), visade framfötterna (showed one's mettle) # REG: Narrative and descriptive contexts # REFL: Han visade sig vara (He turned out to be) # ERR: Confusing the -de ending with -te or forgetting that it is a regular Group 1 verb.
SupiniumNote: GROUP: 1 # FORM: Supinum # FREQ: 9 # NUANCE: Used with 'har' or 'hade' to describe an action that has been completed without a specific time reference or that continues to have relevance. # PART: Har visat upp, har visat hänsyn # COLL: Har visat prov på, har visat stor styrka # REG: Formal and informal use # REFL: Har visat sig (Has appeared/has turned out) # ERR: Mixing up 'visat' with 'visade' when using auxiliary verbs like 'har'.
